meaning: n.咕咕声v.(鸽等)咕咕地叫,唧唧地讲情话
examples: A chief financial officer at a for-profit company may well be able to step into the CEO or COO role at a nonprofit company.
The reason why the company has the titles of "President" and "COO" is to consider the operational capabilities of the senior management.
Anderson would be the COO, and other top Panenergy executives would hold key jobs.
In January, Uniqlo named Larry Meyer, former Forever 21 executive, to the U.S. COO post.
